whoWeAre.intro: Itibá is a culinary and cultural platform born in the Caribbean to rethink gastronomy through identity, structure, and a forward-looking vision. It starts from a deep respect for local products, origin cuisine, and the people who sustain it — producers, cooks, communities — while shaping it with discipline, structure, and international standards.
whoWeAre.connector: Itibá connects three elements that are rarely developed together

pillars.name: Identity
pillars.description: What is ours without folklore, without disguise, without losing its truth.

pillars.name: Operations
pillars.description: Clear processes, real costs, and a cuisine that can grow and sustain itself.

pillars.name: Impact
pillars.description: Education, value chain, and conscious use of territory.

divider.intro: Gastronomy. Territory. Evolution. We create ideas with language, direction, and presence.

origin.title: Origin
origin.intro: Itibá is a creative and gastronomic agency that develops contemporary concepts, experiences, and narratives at the intersection of creative direction, gastronomy, hospitality, territory, and evolution, from the Caribbean and in dialogue with Latin America.
origin.supportText: We create ideas with language, direction, and presence.
origin.blockTitle: An agency that creates from the territory and thinks for the present.
origin.blockText1: Itibá operates at the intersection of creative direction, narrative, gastronomy, and experience. It develops concepts that take shape through activations, events, consulting, collaborations, and platforms with their own identity.
origin.blockText2: Its perspective originates in the Caribbean but is not limited to it. It operates from a Caribbean and Latin American sensibility, open to dialogue between memory, contemporaneity, hospitality, and brand.
origin.projectDesc: An experience where territory, gastronomy, creative direction, and visual language came together in a shared narrative.
origin.clientsText: Itibá collaborates with brands, institutions, hospitality spaces, and cultural partners.

vision.title: Vision
vision.intro: We don’t start from empty formats. We start from ideas with direction.
vision.visionText: At Itibá, vision brings together what we do, how we think about it, and how it takes shape. It is the space where concept, hospitality, gastronomy, and evolution become practice.

creative.title: Creative direction & concept
creative.text: We create foundational concepts, narratives, and creative universes for brands, experiences, platforms, and projects that require a clear identity.
creative.applications: naming, core idea, manifesto, tone, conceptual language, editorial direction, and creative structure.

events.title: Events · experiences · pop-ups
events.text: We design and produce gastronomic, cultural, institutional, and brand experiences with a defined conceptual logic.
events.applications: special dinners, brand activations, launches, immersive experiences, cultural gatherings, and large-scale events.

consulting.title: Gastronomic & cultural consulting
consulting.text: We design and structure proposals for hotels, restaurants, brands, and spaces by integrating concept, gastronomy, and hospitality into identity-driven projects.
consulting.applications: gastronomic conceptualization • territorial narrative • experience • hospitality and project structuring.

education.title: Education & labs
education.text: We create programs, workshops, and labs for teams, institutions, and communities seeking to strengthen capabilities in the gastronomic sector.
education.applications: hospitality workshops, gastronomic creativity, sustainability, and specialized training.

alliances.title: Alliances & integrated experiences
alliances.text: We articulate brand, content, hospitality, and experience in collaborations with cultural meaning.
alliances.applications: strategic alliances, brand integrations, experiential content, and hybrid formats.

vision.thinking: Creativity at Itibá is not an isolated aesthetic resource. It is a way of observing, connecting, and building meaning.

about.intro: Itibá develops concepts, experiences, and strategies rooted in territory, memory, and contemporary transformation. It operates at the intersection of narrative, hospitality, and gastronomy to build projects with identity, depth, and real connection.
about.title: Agency
about.heroText: An agency where gastronomy, creative direction, and evolution work together.
about.heroDesc1: Itibá is a creative and gastronomic agency that develops concepts, experiences, and strategies rooted in territory, memory, and contemporary transformation.
about.heroDesc2: It operates at the intersection of narrative, hospitality, and gastronomy to build projects with identity, depth, and real connection.
about.visionText: We think from the Caribbean and in dialogue with Latin America. This means working with roots, but without static nostalgia; with identity, but without empty folklore; with cultural sensitivity, but also with a contemporary vision.
about.processText: Every project begins with a fundamental question: what needs to take shape, what language can sustain it, and what experience can make it memorable. From there, we articulate concept, narrative, creative direction, experience, and execution.
about.driveText: We are driven by the possibility of transforming memory into language, territory into experience, and intuition into structure. We are interested in creating projects with beauty, yes, but also with thought, intention, and the ability to leave a mark.

members.name: Dayanny De La Cruz
members.title: Chief Executive Officer (CEO)
members.description: Executive leadership, culinary architecture, and social impact vision.
members.quote: Gastronomy is the most honest language that exists.

members.name: Noelia Caccavelli
members.title: Chief Creative Officer (CCO)
members.description: Brand creative vision, storytelling, and development of foundational concepts.
members.quote: Every brand has a story that deserves to be told with precision.

contact.subtitle: Do you have a culinary concept that deserves to shine? We’d love to hear about it.
